trevor-bachmeyer.acute_vs_chronic_note: Dr Bachmeyer often distinguishes acute (injury/infection) vs chronic (longevity/maintenance) dosing. Acute_signals + chronic_signals fields surface candidate contexts; full distillation pending Codex pass.

reset_biology_synthesis.recommendation: Reset Biology suggests asking the practitioner to consider Melanotan II as a multi-receptor melanocortin agonist whose dominant clinical signal in the expert content is risk-adjusted vs Melanotan I, not a stand-alone protocol — Hunter (1961c9375d1784dd 2025-04-09): "I strongly prefer MT-1 over MT-2" and Hunter (0TyW-wlJMxo 00:39:23.920): "I would personally start with Melanotan I if you've never done it before cuz it's much more mild. You can ease yourself into it and then if you say, hey, I want to get darker potentially, you go to Melanotan II." When MT-2 is on the table, we'd ask the prescribing physician to weigh Trevor's pan-receptor mechanism case (Trevor oaBecGSb03Y 00:22:54.720): "Look at the brain. MT2 stimulates melanoorton receptors in the medial preoptic area" and (00:23:16.480): "increases penile erection frequency and intensity across the board by 47%" against the dose-experience caveat that MT-2 darkens moles persistently — Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:53:09.520): "Used 15 mg of Melanotan II in 2 months time. White skin darkened nicely, but moles all over her body also darkened. Now months later, tan is gone, but moles still dark."
reset_biology_synthesis.caveats:
- MT-1 preferred over MT-2 by both Hunter and Taylor: Hunter email threadId 1961c9375d1784dd (2025-04-09): "I strongly prefer MT-1 over MT-2 — yet many in research community still buy MT-2." Taylor (GP0EG8UyvJo 00:05:47.240): "melanotan one is going to be the better peptide to use."
- Persistent mole darkening from MT-2 use: Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:53:09.520): "Used 15 mg of Melanotan II in 2 months time. White skin darkened nicely, but moles all over her body also darkened. Now months later, tan is gone, but moles still dark. Not pretty, especially in face." Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:54:01.160): "moles more dominant with two versus one." Hunter email threadId 1961c9375d1784dd (2025-04-09): "Theoretical melanoma risk + atypical mole reports" and "Darkens freckles, moles, lips."
- Nausea on injection: Taylor (GP0EG8UyvJo 00:05:25.520): "melanotan 2 has some really awful negative side effects of people feeling nauseous sick." Hunter email threadId 1961c9375d1784dd (2025-04-09): "Common: intense nausea (sometimes vomiting)." Hunter (0TyW-wlJMxo 00:38:58.720): "it will make you nauseous" — Hunter notes nausea fades with frequency (00:39:01.760): "the more often more frequently you use it, the less that nausea goes away. It's definitely more prominent in the beginning when you first start injecting."
- Spontaneous prolonged erections in men: Taylor (GP0EG8UyvJo 00:05:31.800): "some men get some erectile issues with it." Hunter email threadId 1961c9375d1784dd (2025-04-09): "Spontaneous prolonged erections (men)." Trevor's mechanism backing (oaBecGSb03Y 00:23:14.159): "increases penile erection frequency and intensity across the board by 47%" — clinically real, dose-dependent.
- Orange-tone uneven pigmentation: Taylor (GP0EG8UyvJo 00:05:38.960): "melanotan 2 will also make people Orange." Hunter email threadId 1961c9375d1784dd (2025-04-09): "Tan often uneven, unnatural-looking" and "Speckled/uneven pigmentation possible."
- Anxiety + appetite suppression from broad receptor activation: Hunter email threadId 1961c9375d1784dd (2025-04-09): "Flushing, appetite suppression" and "Anxiety, restlessness from broad receptor activity."
- FDA Category-2 removal (regulatory caveat): Hunter email threadId 2026-04-16 fda-peptide-announcement-pcac-503a: "April 15, 2026 — FDA Removes 12 Peptides from Category 2" with "Melanotan II" listed; PCAC review pending July 2026.
- Trevor explicitly refuses to give a dosing number for tanning: Trevor (oaBecGSb03Y 00:04:14.080): "what's my dosage for tanning? Which is makes me insane when people ask that" and (00:04:24.720): "the right question is what happens when you solve the melanoorton system?" — meaning RB should not invent a number from his content; defer to Hunter/Taylor for protocol when MT-2 is chosen anyway.
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_consensus: Quoted overlap on hierarchy and side-effect ceiling: Hunter (0TyW-wlJMxo 00:39:23.920): "I would personally start with Melanotan I if you've never done it before cuz it's much more mild"; Taylor (G27dIPBJUSs 00:39:38.760): "I would personally start with Melanotan 1 if you've never done it before, cuz it's much more mild. You can ease yourself into it. And then, if you say, hey, I want to get darker, potentially, you go to Melanotan 2." Both Hunter and Taylor independently flag MT-2 nausea + erectile effects as the recurring downsides that drive their MT-1 default — Taylor (GP0EG8UyvJo 00:05:23.520): "melanotan 2 has some really awful negative side effects of people feeling nauseous sick um some men get some erectile issues with it" matches Hunter email threadId 1961c9375d1784dd (2025-04-09): "Common: intense nausea... Spontaneous prolonged erections (men)." Trevor agrees the receptor-arousal mechanism is real (oaBecGSb03Y 00:23:14.159): "direct melano cordon agonism in the PVN increases penile erection frequency and intensity across the board by 47%" — the same biology Hunter and Taylor describe as a side effect, Trevor reframes as a primary therapeutic effect. Cross-expert agreement that MT-2 hits multiple melanocortin receptors: Hunter email threadId 1961c9375d1784dd (2025-04-09): "MT-2 = shorter, cyclic peptide that hits MULTIPLE melanocortin receptors — also binds MC-3 + MC-4 → widespread effects, good/bad/unpredictable"; Trevor (oaBecGSb03Y 00:01:23.439): "you can't selectively choose what it does."
reset_biology_synthesis.cross_expert_disagreement: Framing differs sharply. Hunter and Taylor frame MT-2 as a tanning peptide with worse risk/reward than MT-1 — Hunter email threadId 1961c9375d1784dd (2025-04-09): "If your goal is a beautiful, healthy tan—without nausea, intense side effects, or unnecessary risks—Melanotan 1 injections at 250-500 mcg, 2-3 times weekly, should be your peptide of choice." Taylor (GP0EG8UyvJo 00:05:47.240): "melanotan one is going to be the better peptide to use." Trevor (oaBecGSb03Y 00:00:38.960): "most people think it's a tanning peptide and a couple of people think it's a libido peptide. You're not wrong. However, that's like saying water is for drinking. You're missing everything else that it's good for" — Trevor frames MT-2 as a system-restoration peptide hitting MC1/MC3/MC4/MC5 with metabolic, immune, neurological, and cardiovascular effects (oaBecGSb03Y 00:11:41.440): "metabolic tissues. MT2 hits MC4 receptors in the sympathetic nervous system which increases metabolic rate. But and this is very important. It does this while at the same time improving insulin signaling through EMPK activation muscle tissues." Dosing posture also differs: Hunter (0TyW-wlJMxo 00:38:43.359) and Taylor (G27dIPBJUSs) both volunteer a starter protocol of "I like to start with 250 micrograms... once a week. You can go up to two to three times per week" (Hunter clarifies he is starting back his MT-1 here at 00:38:28.440 "I'm starting back my MT-1" — not strictly an MT-2 protocol but the Q&A frame applies to the tanning category). Trevor refuses to volunteer a tanning dose at all (oaBecGSb03Y 00:04:14.080): "what's my dosage for tanning? Which is makes me insane when people ask that." Cancer-history caution is implicit in Hunter's mole-darkening warnings (1961c9375d1784dd 2025-04-09: "Theoretical melanoma risk + atypical mole reports") and absent from Trevor's masterclass — Trevor does not address melanoma/mole risk at all, while Hunter and Taylor make it the lead caveat.

chronic.dose: Hunter (0TyW-wlJMxo 00:38:43.359): "I like to start with 250 micrograms" with build-up to (00:38:54.520): "I would do 1 mg per day. But I would build up to that cuz if Build it up cuz it will make you nauseous." Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:53:09.520) reports observed self-experiment dose: "Used 15 mg of Melanotan II in 2 months time" (≈250 mcg/day cumulative).
chronic.route: Hunter (0TyW-wlJMxo 00:39:08.359): "when you first start injecting" — subcutaneous injection implied. Hunter email threadId 1961c9375d1784dd (2025-04-09): "250-500 mcg subcutaneously" (MT-1 protocol; carried-over implication for MT-2 since both are SubQ peptides per cyclic-peptide context). Trevor (oaBecGSb03Y) does not address route specifically for MT-2.
chronic.frequency: Hunter (0TyW-wlJMxo 00:38:46.000): "Since your first time, just do it once a week. You can go up to two to three times per week and then as you go from there, you can scale up the dose." Hunter (0TyW-wlJMxo 00:39:11.480): "three times a week um you can use it. Also it's really beneficial to use it the days that you are going to go out and get sun exposure."
chronic.duration: No expert volunteers a fixed cycle for MT-2. Hunter (E7GPNidicqY 01:53:09.520) describes a 2-month course: "15 mg of Melanotan II in 2 months time" with persistent mole darkening as a downside — implicit caution against indefinite/repeat cycles. Trevor refuses to volunteer duration: (oaBecGSb03Y 00:04:14.080): "what's my dosage for tanning? Which is makes me insane when people ask that."
chronic.verbatim_citation: Hunter (0TyW-wlJMxo 00:38:43.359): "I like to start with 250 micrograms. Since your first time, just do it once a week. You can go up to two to three times per week and then as you go from there, you can scale up the dose. Then for instance like if I'm in the beach in the summer, I would do 1 mg per day. But I would build up to that cuz if Build it up cuz it will make you nauseous." Followed by Hunter (0TyW-wlJMxo 00:39:23.920): "And I would I mean I have no problem with people taking Melanotan II. I would personally start with Melanotan I if you've never done it before cuz it's much more mild."
chronic.timestamp: 00:38:43.359 (250mcg start) · 00:38:54.520 (1mg beach build-up) · 00:39:11.480 (2-3x/week) · 00:39:23.920 (MT-2 framing). Hunter caveat episode: https://youtu.be/E7GPNidicqY 01:53:09.520 (15mg/2mo + mole darkening).

reset_biology_synthesis.female_specific_notes: Taylor frames MT-2 as the inferior choice for women specifically due to side-effect profile and aesthetic mismatch — Taylor (GP0EG8UyvJo 00:05:23.520): "melanotan 2 has some really awful negative side effects of people feeling nauseous sick um some men get some erectile issues with it um and melanotan 2 will also make people Orange um so yeah I don't want to look orange um so melanotan one is going to be the better peptide to use." Taylor (G27dIPBJUSs 00:39:38.760): "I would personally start with Melanotan 1 if you've never done it before, cuz it's much more mild." Taylor mentions her own MT-1 use and links pigmentation peptides to broader hormonal-transition support (GP0EG8UyvJo 00:05:13 surrounding context): "helped me so much when I was going through um anxiety and coming off birth control and dealing with a lot of hormonal changes" — that benefit is attributed to MT-1, not MT-2. No Taylor content surfaced in the top sources speaks to MT-2 in pregnancy, fertility, or post-menopause directly; defer to MT-1 card for those contexts.
